title: Tracking Pixel Reconnaissance
overview: Tracking pixel is a tiny (typically 1x1) invisible image embedded in emails to silently collect information once the recipient opens the message. While commonly used in marketing, attackers use it for reconnaissance in phishing campaigns.

Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.
